input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android and Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (for Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires certain VS components:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want Build Tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workloads via the VS Installer CLI:

```powershell
# Discover available workload IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  export --channelId VisualStudio.17.Release

# Install the needed components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\setup.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android build dependency)

```powershell
# Discover available OpenJDK versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by current Android Gradle tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version
```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

```powershell
# Install Android Studio (includes SDK Manager) — or standalone CLI tools
winget install Google.AndroidStudio

# --- OR standalone command-line tools only: ---
# Download cmdline-tools from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only
# Unzip to e.g. C:\Android\cmdline-tools\latest

# Set env vars
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Add tools to PATH
$sdkManager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover available platforms and build-tools dynamically
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -First 10
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis" | Select-Object -First 5

# Install required components (adjust versions from discovery above)
& $sdkManager "platform-tools" `
              "platforms;android-35" `
              "build-tools;35.0.0" `
              "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
              "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkManager --licenses
```

## 6. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdManager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdManager list device

# Create an AVD (pick a device from the list above)
& $avdManager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_7"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 7. Enable Windows Developer Mode (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Check current state
Get-WindowsDeveloperLicense

# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 8. Create and Run a MAUI Project

```powershell
# See available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 9. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Quick health check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
java -version
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0-*` with whatever `dotnet --version` reports (e.g., `net10.0-*` for .NET 10).
- The `--list` commands in steps 5 and 6 let you **discover versions dynamically** rather than hardcoding them.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled in Windows Features for Android emulator hardware acceleration (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
